name: Allariz - Penamá Peak Loop
name_original: Reserva de la Biosfera de Allariz – circular desde Allariz por A Portela y O San Salvador
description: Hike the difficult 11.0-mile Reserva de la Biosfera de Allariz circular trail, exploring ancient forests, historic villages, and the Arnoia
region_intro: Hiking around Allariz offers diverse landscapes shaped by the Arnoia River and the Allariz granitic massif to the north. The region is part of the UNESCO Allariz Area Biosphere Reserve, featuring varied ecosystems including river plains, cultivated areas, and mixed forests of oak and chestnut trees. These natural features provide a range of terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Allariz The most popular hiking route is Allariz - Penamá Peak Loop, a 11.0 miles (17.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 3 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends through varied terrain, potentially offering views of the granitic massif. Another top favourite among local hikers is Allariz loop from Allariz, a moderate 9.6 miles (15.4 km) path. This trail explores the surroundings of Allariz, often following the natural contours of the river landscape. Local hikers also love the Ximpa – Fonte do Santo loop from Allariz, a 9.7 miles (15.6 km) trail leading through the diverse landscapes of the Biosphere Reserve, often completed in about 4 hours 45 minutes. Hiking around Allariz is defined by riverine landscapes, diverse forest ecosystems, and the granitic massif. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Allariz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore Allariz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Allariz?
A: Allariz offers a good selection of routes, with over 20 hiking trails available. These routes cater to various difficulty levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging ascents into the granitic massif.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Allariz?
A: Yes, Allariz has several easy hiking options perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31888565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Allariz Roman Bridge – Allariz loop from Allariz&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.3-mile (6.9 km) trail that takes just over an hour to complete, offering a gentle introduction to the area's beauty.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Allariz?
A: Many of the hiking routes in Allariz are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Allariz Roman Bridge – Nanín Fountain and Washhouse loop from Allariz&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.6-mile (10.7 km) loop that explores the local area.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on hikes in Allariz?
A: Hiking in Allariz offers diverse natural features. You'll encounter the picturesque Arnoia River, lush forests of chestnut and oak trees, and the granitic massif to the north. The region is also home to highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3829291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Boutureira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the historic Vilanova Bridge crossing the Arnoia.

Q: Is Allariz part of a protected natural area?
A: Yes, Allariz is located within the UNESCO Allariz Area Biosphere Reserve, designated since 2005. This reserve spans over 21,000 hectares and is recognized for its diverse landscapes, including river plains, cultivated areas, and mixed forests, which support a wide array of flora and fauna. You can learn more about the reserve on the &lt;a href="https://www.unesco.org/en/mab/area-de-allariz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;UNESCO website&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Allariz suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails in Allariz are suitable for families, especially the easier routes along the Arnoia River. These riverside walks offer pleasant, relatively flat terrain and opportunities to enjoy nature, including riverside beaches like Acearrica, Campo de Vilanova, and Arnado.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Allariz?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in Allariz, allowing you to explore the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to follow local regulations regarding pet waste.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Allariz?
A: The best time to hike in Allariz is typically during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. Spring brings blooming flora, while autumn showcases beautiful fall foliage in the chestnut and oak forests. Summer can also be pleasant, especially for riverside walks, but it can get warm.

Q: Are there options for public transport to access hiking trails in Allariz?
A: While Allariz is well-connected by road, public transport options directly to trailheads might be limited. It's recommended to check local bus schedules for routes to Allariz town, and then plan your hikes from there, as many routes start directly from the town center.

Q: Where can I park when planning a hike in Allariz?
A: Allariz, being a charming town, offers various parking options, particularly around the historic center and near popular access points to the Arnoia River. Look for designated parking areas within the town, which often provide convenient starting points for many of the local hiking routes.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Allariz?
A: The hiking experience in Allariz is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the riverine environment, and the blend of natural beauty with historical charm.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions to explore near the trails?
A: Absolutely. Allariz boasts a rich historical-artistic complex. Beyond the trails, you can explore the medieval historic quarter with its narrow streets and squares. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3004027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Freixo Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the Romanesque church of Santa María de Vilanova are notable historical structures often encountered on riverside walks. Additionally, the Ecospace of Rexo, an artistic installation harmonizing with nature, is located just east of Allariz.

Q: Can I swim in the Arnoia River while hiking?
A: Yes, the Arnoia River in Allariz is known for being clean and safe for swimming. There are several riverside beaches, such as Acearrica, Campo de Vilanova, and Arnado, where you can take a refreshing dip during your hike, especially on warmer days.
tips: Regarding the current work, although modified, we must attribute a Romanesque origin to it and it is known that in the High Middle Ages it was in the custody of the Knights of the Order of Saint John of Jerusalem.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This circular route is generally classified as having a medium to high difficulty, or a 'Hard hike' according to some sources. It covers approximately 17.7 km and requires very good physical fitness. While the paths are easily accessible in terms of navigation, the length and elevation gain contribute to its challenging rating.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable features. You'll start near the historic Convento de Santa Clara in Allariz and quickly enter the lush 'O Canelar' forest, a protected core zone of the Biosphere Reserve. Other highlights include the Allariz Roman Bridge, the Bridge over the Arnoia River, and A Fonte do Santo, a miraculous spring. You'll also pass through San Salvador dos Penedos, one of the first abandoned villages in the region.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Allariz?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts in the historic town of Allariz, often near the Convento de Santa Clara. Allariz offers various parking options within the town, and it's advisable to look for public parking areas close to the town center or the starting point of the hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Biosphere Reserve is a natural area, specific regulations for dogs on this particular trail are not detailed. Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in Galicia, but it's always best to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Allariz Biosphere Reserve offers beautiful scenery year-round. However, spring and autumn are often ideal for hiking due to milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summers can be warm, so an early start is recommended if hiking during those months. Winters are generally mild but can be wet.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike or the Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to enter the Reserva de la Biosfera de Allariz. It is a UNESCO-designated area, but access to its natural trails is generally free and open to the public.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse range of terrain. The trail begins on historic paths in Allariz, then transitions into dense native forests like 'O Canelar'. You'll also traverse meadows, orchards, and small wooded areas, often on old local paths known as 'corgas' or 'corredoiras'. Expect some ancient stone paths and varied surfaces throughout.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Camiño Xabrés por Verín (30%) and the Mtb Camiño Xabrés por Verín (30%). Smaller portions also overlap with Paicordeiro-Penama, A Costiña, and various local paths like Camiño a Paicordeiro and Camiño de Fontes.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter in the Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: As part of a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, the area boasts rich biodiversity. While specific sightings are never guaranteed, you might encounter various bird species, small mammals, and diverse insect life. The protected 'O Canelar' forest is particularly rich in native flora and fauna.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its classification as a 'difficult' or 'hard' hike, covering nearly 18 km with significant elevation changes, this specific circular route is generally not recommended for young children or families seeking an easy outing. However, Allariz offers over 100 km of hiking and cycling routes, so shorter, less demanding options may be available in the wider area.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this challenging hike, you should pack plenty of water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, and weather-appropriate clothing (layers are recommended). A map or GPS device (like Komoot's app) is advisable, along with sun protection and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles can also be helpful for stability on varied terrain.
